title: Garlicky Crispy Peruvian Lamb Bowl
description: A flavorful Peruvian-inspired dish featuring crispy lamb leg, crispy potatoes, and a side of spicy slaw.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix together Korean chili flakes, garlic powder, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the lamb leg, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat 2 tablespoons of corn oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb leg for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and roast the lamb leg for 20 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 63°C.
While the lamb is cooking, peel the potatoes and cut them into 1-inch wedges.
Heat 2 tablespoons of corn oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat.
Add the potato wedges to the saucepan and fry until crispy, about 5-7 minutes.
Drain the potatoes on paper towels and season with salt.
In a small bowl, mix together lime juice and chopped cilantro.
In a separate bowl, mix together chopped red onion and lime juice mixture.
To assemble the dish, place a portion of crispy potatoes in the bottom of a Korean brass bowl, followed by a slice of roasted lamb leg, and finally a spoonful of spicy slaw.
Garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
